nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving streams, shallow waters, ditches, ponds, and oxbow lakes

summary: A classic feathery-leaved South American stem plant with finely dichotomously-branched foliage; a 1980s-90s aquascape staple that thrives best with high light and CO2 supplementation.
careNotes: Cabomba aquatica is the canonical hobby Cabomba — a stem plant from slow-moving South American freshwaters with delicate, feathery, dichotomously-branched leaves arranged in opposite pairs. Stems can reach 30–80 cm. This entry covers the genus-level 'Cabomba' for the AquaMate manifest; the related Cabomba caroliniana (Carolina Fanwort) is recorded as a separate entry. Source disagreement on demands: Tropica rates lighting as medium ('least demanding of the Cabomba-species') and CO2 as low; Buce Plant calls for moderate-to-high light and recommends CO2; aquariumbreeder.com and most hobbyist references describe sustained healthy growth as requiring high light (≈40–50 PAR) plus CO2 injection, with stems shedding lower leaves under weaker conditions. Following the manifest's guidance and the majority of references, this entry treats lighting as high and CO2 as recommended; without both, growth is generally reported as leggy, with lower-stem dieback and rapid leaf shedding. Growth rate under good conditions has been reported as fast (~2.5 cm per day). Stems are notably brittle — handle gently when planting or trimming, and avoid disturbing established stands. The plant is sensitive to sudden parameter swings and is prone to melt if water hardness, pH, or temperature changes abruptly. Generally advised to keep around 22–26 °C in soft, slightly acidic water (pH ≈ 6.5, gH 3–8). Mostly a water-column feeder, so liquid fertilisation tends to outperform root tabs alone; substrate type is less critical than column nutrients. Best appearance is achieved when planted in dense groups in the midground or background.
breedingNotes: Propagated by terminal stem cuttings (4–12 cm); remove the lowest few centimetres of leaves and replant gently into the substrate. Cuttings root easily under stable conditions but are prone to damage if handled roughly. Lateral side-shoots from mature stems can also be separated and replanted.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Manifest-supplied Tropica search URL was resolved to the species page at plantdetails/4431/4431. Tropica rates Cabomba aquatica as easy, medium light demand, low CO2 demand, fast growth.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing, lighting, CO2, placement, and propagation. Buce Plant calls for moderate-to-high light and recommends CO2 for best growth; lists pH 6.0–7.5.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (aquariumbreeder.com)
additional.notes: Used for temperature (20–28 °C), gH (3–8), high-light requirement (~40–50 PAR), CO2 recommendation, fast growth (≈2.5 cm/day), and brittleness note. Rates care as moderate, which together with the manifest's high-light/CO2 guidance supported promoting careLevel to intermediate vs the easy rating from Tropica/Buce.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature range (68–82 °F), pH (6.0–7.5), maximum height ≈ 31 in (~80 cm), and South American native range.
